message:
fix(server,db,storage): adopt recovery-aware SQLite opener across all connection sites, add TUI poller auto-recovery, fix cache metrics and coalescer stats

Recovery-aware SQLite opener (all TUI/server modules):
Replace all DbConn::open_file calls with open_sqlite_file_with_recovery
across 12 call sites: lib.rs (dashboard stats), mail_ui.rs (recipe loading),
tool_metrics.rs, tui_app.rs (rethread, palette agent metadata, recent
messages), and all 6 TUI screens (attachments, explorer, messages,
reservations, search, threads). This ensures that every connection attempt
first checks file health and attempts automatic recovery from backups
before opening, eliminating a class of crash-on-corrupt-file scenarios
in the TUI that previously required manual intervention.

Export new public API: is_sqlite_recovery_error_message and
open_sqlite_file_with_recovery from the pool module.

TUI poller auto-recovery (tui_poller.rs):
Add maybe_attempt_sqlite_recovery function with rate limiting (15s minimum
interval per path via static POLLER_RECOVERY_GATES HashMap). The
DbStatQueryBatcher now carries the sqlite path and calls handle_query_error
on every failed query to detect corruption/malformation patterns and
trigger recovery. open_sync_connection refactored to
open_sync_connection_with_path returning both path and connection, with
recovery-on-open-failure semantics.

Cache metrics accuracy (cache.rs):
Record cache miss metrics on early None returns from get_project,
get_project_by_human_key, get_agent_scoped, and get_agent_by_id_scoped.
Previously misses were only tracked on TTL expiry, under-counting the
actual miss rate.

Coalescer stats fix (storage/lib.rs):
coalescer_commit_batch now returns (num_commits, batched_items) tuple
instead of just a count, fixing sequential commit paths that incorrectly
incremented the commit counter by 1 regardless of actual batch size.

Lock healing robustness (storage/lib.rs):
heal_archive_locks reworked to handle Windows-style unlock-before-delete:
tries remove_file while holding the lock first, falls back to unlocking
then removing on PermissionDenied.

Also: rustfmt formatting across pool.rs, s3fifo.rs, two_tier.rs,
cleanup.rs, build_slots.rs, reservation_index.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -834,7 +834,7 @@
 {"id":"br-2qycc","title":"T8.2: Implement spatial navigation and focus memory","description":"Wire the focus graphs (T8.1) to keyboard handling so arrow keys move focus spatially
and focus state persists per screen.

SPATIAL NAVIGATION LOGIC:
- When arrow key pressed and current widget doesn't consume it:
  1. Get current focus node's Rect position
  2. Find neighbor in arrow direction (nearest node by distance)
  3. Move focus to that neighbor
  4. Highlight newly focused panel

FOCUS MEMORY:
- Each screen model stores last_focused_node: Option<FocusNodeId>
- On screen entry: restore last_focused_node (or default to first node)
- On screen exit: save current focus node

KEYBINDING PRIORITY:
- Widget-level bindings take priority (e.g., arrow keys in a list scroll the list)
- Focus navigation only activates when widget doesn't consume the arrow key
- Alternative: use Ctrl+Arrow for focus navigation (always available)

FILES: tui_app.rs, tui_screens/*.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Ctrl+Arrow moves focus between panels spatially
- [ ] Focus memory restored on screen switch
- [ ] Focus highlight visually distinct (panel_border_focused)
- [ ] No conflict with widget-level arrow key bindings
- [ ] Works with dock layout changes

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Implemented spatial focus navigation + focus memory in TUI app. Added global Ctrl+Arrow routing (respects text-input consumption), persisted per-screen focus on transitions/deep-links, and rendered focused-panel outline using panel_border_focused. Also adjusted focus-graph neighbor scoring to prefer orthogonally-overlapping panels before distance so directional hops are intuitive (e.g., Messages detail <- left -> list). Validation: (1) cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --all-targets (pass), (2) cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib focus_graph_messages_neighbors_match_spatial_layout (pass), (3) cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib ctrl_arrow_moves_focus_between_panels_spatially (pass), (4) cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib focus_memory_restores_previous_target_when_returning_to_screen (pass), (5) cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib plain_arrow_keys_do_not_trigger_global_spatial_focus_move (pass). rch remote runs attempted first but fail on workers due missing sibling path dependencies (../frankentui etc.), so tests/check used documented local fallback.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyElm","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T18:13:36.224933427Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T18:13:36.224907258Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["focus","navigation","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2qycc","depends_on_id":"br-291fs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:32.105986677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2qycc","depends_on_id":"br-3nbef","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:12.978666991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2r1f","title":"T1.1: Define gate configuration data model (GateConfig, GateResult, GateReport structs)","description":"## Objective
Define the canonical data contracts for `am ci` so all downstream gate execution and reporting logic shares one typed schema.

## Work
- Specify `GateConfig`, `GateResult`, and `GateReport` structures with explicit field semantics.
- Encode gate identity, command context, timing, status, and diagnostics payload boundaries.
- Document serialization expectations for stable `--json` output and future extension points.

## Deliverable
A stable, reviewed type model that serves as the source-of-truth contract for the native CI command path.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, error taxonomy, and exit-code behavior for downstream implementation.
- Captures parity mapping to legacy behavior, including intentional deviations and technical rationale.
- Includes a concrete validation strategy (unit + integration + e2e) and detailed logging/artifact expectations for downstream tasks.
- Is self-contained and executable without referring back to external markdown plan documents.
- Leaves no ambiguous requirements that would permit incompatible implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:15.566210711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:27.119418770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:27.119397470Z","close_reason":"Implemented GateConfig, GateResult, GateReport, and supporting types in ci.rs (921 lines). Includes all 13 default gates, comprehensive unit tests, and JSON serialization.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"br-2r1f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T1.1: Define Gate Configuration Data Model

## What to build
Create the data structures that represent CI gates, their results, and the final report.
These replace the implicit schema encoded in the jq pipeline of scripts/ci.sh (lines 103-276).

## Structs needed

```rust
/// A single CI gate definition
struct GateConfig {
    name: String,
    category: GateCategory,  // quality, performance, security, docs
    command: Vec<String>,     // e.g. ["cargo", "fmt", "--all", "--", "--check"]
    skip_in_quick: bool,      // true for E2E gates
    parallel_group: Option<String>,  // gates in same group can run concurrently
}

/// Result of running a single gate
struct GateResult {
    name: String,
    category: GateCategory,
    status: GateStatus,       // pass, fail, skip
    elapsed: Duration,
    command: String,
    stderr_tail: Option<String>,  // last N lines of stderr on failure
}

/// The full gate report (schema: am_ci_gate_report.v1)
struct GateReport {
    schema_version: String,   // "am_ci_gate_report.v1"
    generated_at: String,     // ISO-8601
    mode: RunMode,            // full, quick
    decision: Decision,       // go, no-go
    decision_reason: String,
    release_eligible: bool,
    summary: GateSummary,     // total/pass/fail/skip counts
    thresholds: HashMap<GateCategory, ThresholdInfo>,
    gate_logic: GateLogicInfo,
    gates: Vec<GateResult>,
}
```

## Implementation notes
- All structs derive Serialize/Deserialize for JSON output
- The schema must be compatible with existing gate_report.json consumers
- GateCategory enum: Quality, Performance, Security, Docs
- GateStatus enum: Pass, Fail, Skip
- Decision enum: Go, NoGo
- Include the 13 default gates from ci.sh as a built-in DEFAULT_GATES constant

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (new file, top section)

## Reference
scripts/ci.sh lines 78-116 (gate/skip_gate functions), lines 199-276 (jq report schema)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:28:08Z"},{"id":244,"issue_id":"br-2r1f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION from deep audit

The exact 13 gates (with categories and commands) must be hardcoded as DEFAULT_GATES:

QUALITY (8):
1. "Format check" → cargo fmt --all -- --check
2. "Clippy" → cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
3. "Build workspace" → cargo build --workspace
4. "Unit + integration tests" → cargo test --workspace
5. "Mode matrix harness" → cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test mode_matrix_harness -- --nocapture
6. "Semantic conformance" → cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test semantic_conformance -- --nocapture
7. "Help snapshots" → cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test help_snapshots -- --nocapture
8-9. "E2E dual-mode" + "E2E mode matrix" → bash scripts (skip_in_quick=true)

PERFORMANCE (1):
10. "Perf + security regressions" → cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test perf_security_regressions -- --nocapture

SECURITY (1):
11. "E2E security/privacy" → bash tests/e2e/test_security_privacy.sh (skip_in_quick=true)

DOCS (1):
12. "Release docs references present" → bash -c 'test -f docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md && ...'

QUALITY (cont.):
13. "E2E TUI accessibility" → bash scripts/e2e_tui_a11y.sh (skip_in_quick=true)

Environment variables to set on child processes via Command::env():
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR = ${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-/data/tmp/cargo-target}
- DATABASE_URL = sqlite:///tmp/ci_local.sqlite3
- STORAGE_ROOT = /tmp/ci_storage
- AGENT_NAME = CiLocalAgent
- HTTP_HOST = 127.0.0.1
- HTTP_PORT = 1
- HTTP_PATH = /mcp/

Use Command::env() to set these on child processes, NOT std::env::set_var (which is unsafe in Rust 2024).

 {"id":"br-2rern","title":"R5.1: Implement am robot agents — agent roster with activity status (active/idle/inactive), program, model","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:27.548546766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:29:01.785491088Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:29:01.785462445Z","close_reason":"Robot agents: agent roster with active/idle/inactive status, program, model, msg_count, --active filter, --sort support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2rern","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:56.742249770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":318,"issue_id":"br-2rern","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R5.1: `am robot agents`

## What
Agent roster with activity indicators. Shows who's active, idle, or inactive — equivalent to the TUI Agents screen.

## Data Collection
```sql
SELECT a.name, a.program, a.model, a.last_active_at,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages WHERE from_agent = a.name AND project_id = a.project_id) AS msg_count
FROM agents a
WHERE a.project_id = ?
ORDER BY a.last_active_at DESC
```

## Status Classification
- **active**: `last_active_at` within 15 minutes of now
- **idle**: `last_active_at` between 15 minutes and 2 hours ago
- **inactive**: `last_active_at` more than 2 hours ago
- Note: `last_active_at` is stored as i64 (microseconds since epoch)

## Flags
- `--active` — Only show active agents
- `--sort <col>` — Sort by column: last_active (default), name, msg_count, program

## Output Format
```
agents[4]{name,program,model,last_active,msg_count,status}:
  BlueLake,claude-code,opus-4.6,5m ago,45,active
  GreenCastle,claude-code,sonnet-4.5,30m ago,67,idle
  RedFox,codex-cli,gpt-5.2,2h ago,23,idle
  SilverWolf,gemini-cli,gemini-2.5,6h ago,12,inactive

summary:
  total: 4
  active: 1
  idle: 2
  inactive: 1
```

## Relative Time Formatting
Convert `last_active_at` (microseconds) to relative string:
- < 1 minute: "just now"
- < 60 minutes: "{N}m ago"
- < 24 hours: "{N}h ago"
- < 7 days: "{N}d ago"
- else: ISO-8601 date

## Acceptance Criteria
- All agents listed with correct status classification
- --active filter works
- --sort changes ordering
- Message count accurate
- Relative time formatting correct
- Empty agent list produces clean output
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:26Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-2rfq","title":"T10.3: Cut CI/release workflows over to native command execution paths","description":"## Objective
Cut CI and release gate execution paths over to native commands as tracks complete.

## Work
- Update workflow jobs and local CI instructions to invoke native command paths.
- Preserve artifact and exit-code semantics expected by governance checks.
- Add temporary dual-run/equivalence checks where risk is high.

## Deliverable
CI/release processes consume native commands as primary implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:58.794427364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:53.238984587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","migration","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2rfq","depends_on_id":"br-20qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:18.266288363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2rfq","depends_on_id":"br-246y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:18.478730886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2rfq","depends_on_id":"br-3rls","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:12.302901886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2rfq","depends_on_id":"br-84gq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:17.836557020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2rfq","depends_on_id":"br-h1yu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:18.051539345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2rn5b","title":"R5.4: Implement am robot attachments — attachment inventory with type, size, provenance, storage mode","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:28.197860224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:31:03.983656602Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:31:03.983636124Z","close_reason":"Robot attachments: parses JSON attachments array from messages, reports type/size/sender/subject/message_id","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2rn5b","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:57.426676883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"br-2rn5b","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R5.4: `am robot attachments`

## What
Attachment inventory with provenance. Shows what files have been shared, by whom, in what context.

## Data Collection
Attachments are stored alongside messages. Query:
```sql
SELECT a.content_type, a.size_bytes, m.from_agent, m.subject, m.id AS message_id, p.slug AS project
FROM attachments a
JOIN messages m ON a.message_id = m.id
JOIN projects p ON m.project_id = p.id
ORDER BY a.id DESC
LIMIT ?
```

Note: Check the actual schema — attachments may be stored differently (inline in message body, as separate files, or in a dedicated table). Adapt the query accordingly.

## Output Format
```
attachments[5]{type,size,sender,subject,message_id,project}:
  image/webp,45KB,BlueLake,Screenshot of auth flow,201,backend-api
  text/plain,2KB,RedFox,Error log excerpt,198,backend-api
  image/png,120KB,GreenCastle,DB schema diagram,180,shared-lib
  application/json,8KB,BlueLake,API spec draft,175,backend-api
  text/markdown,3KB,SilverWolf,Meeting notes,160,frontend-app

total_size: 178KB
by_type: image/webp(1),text/plain(1),image/png(1),application/json(1),text/markdown(1)
```

## Size Formatting
- < 1KB: "{N}B"
- < 1MB: "{N}KB" (round to nearest KB)
- < 1GB: "{N}MB" (round to 1 decimal)
- else: "{N}GB"

## Acceptance Criteria
- All attachments listed with provenance (sender, message, project)
- Size formatted in human-readable units
- by_type aggregation correct
- total_size is sum of all attachment sizes
- Works with 0 attachments (clean empty output)
- Handles messages without attachments gracefully

 {"id":"br-2ro9j","title":"R3.5: Unit tests for thread, search, message, navigate commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:10.469208574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:19:50.126935538Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:19:50.126862562Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","depends_on_id":"br-2sovq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:51.764875215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","depends_on_id":"br-3d9wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:52.534304498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","depends_on_id":"br-3hfrw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:52.055580690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","depends_on_id":"br-dcnr4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:52.295284020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":312,"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.5: Track 3 Unit Tests

## What
Unit tests for all 4 context & discovery commands. Test data retrieval, formatting, edge cases.

## Test Cases (minimum 18 tests)

### robot thread (5 tests)
1. `test_thread_markdown_format` — verify headers, separators, message blocks
2. `test_thread_toon_format` — tabular messages, bodies omitted by default
3. `test_thread_include_bodies_toon` — --include-bodies adds body field
4. `test_thread_participants_deduped` — participants list has no duplicates
5. `test_thread_single_message` — thread with 1 message renders correctly

### robot search (5 tests)
6. `test_search_relevance_ordering` — results sorted by bm25 score
7. `test_search_facets` — by_thread, by_agent, by_importance computed correctly
8. `test_search_snippets` — snippets contain query terms
9. `test_search_importance_filter` — --importance high filters correctly
10. `test_search_no_results` — empty result set produces clean output

### robot message (5 tests)
11. `test_message_full_context` — body, sender info, thread position all present
12. `test_message_previous_next` — adjacent message summaries correct
13. `test_message_first_in_thread` — no previous, next present
14. `test_message_last_in_thread` — previous present, no next
15. `test_message_with_attachments` — attachment list rendered

### robot navigate (3 tests)
16. `test_navigate_inbox` — resource://inbox/Agent resolves to inbox data
17. `test_navigate_invalid_uri` — malformed URI produces helpful error
18. `test_navigate_all_patterns` — every supported URI pattern parses correctly

## Test Setup
- Seed DB with: 1 project, 3 agents, 15 messages across 3 threads, 2 attachments
- FTS5 index populated for search tests
- Use `open_db_sync()` pattern

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 18+ tests pass
- Tests use real DB (not mocked)
- FTS tests verify actual FTS5 functionality
- Edge cases (empty thread, no results, first/last message) covered
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:24Z"},{"id":337,"issue_id":"br-2ro9j","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.5: Track 3 Unit Tests

## What
Unit tests for all 4 context & discovery commands. Test data retrieval, formatting, edge cases.

## Test Cases (minimum 18 tests)

### robot thread (5 tests)
1. `test_thread_markdown_format` — verify headers, separators, message blocks
2. `test_thread_toon_format` — tabular messages, bodies omitted by default
3. `test_thread_include_bodies_toon` — --include-bodies adds body field
4. `test_thread_participants_deduped` — participants list has no duplicates
5. `test_thread_single_message` — thread with 1 message renders correctly

### robot search (5 tests)
6. `test_search_relevance_ordering` — results sorted by bm25 score
7. `test_search_facets` — by_thread, by_agent, by_importance computed correctly
8. `test_search_snippets` — snippets contain query terms
9. `test_search_importance_filter` — --importance high filters correctly
10. `test_search_no_results` — empty result set produces clean output

### robot message (5 tests)
11. `test_message_full_context` — body, sender info, thread position all present
12. `test_message_previous_next` — adjacent message summaries correct
13. `test_message_first_in_thread` — no previous, next present
14. `test_message_last_in_thread` — previous present, no next
15. `test_message_with_attachments` — attachment list rendered

### robot navigate (3 tests)
16. `test_navigate_inbox` — resource://inbox/Agent resolves to inbox data
17. `test_navigate_invalid_uri` — malformed URI produces helpful error
18. `test_navigate_all_patterns` — every supported URI pattern parses correctly

## Test Setup
- Seed DB with: 1 project, 3 agents, 15 messages across 3 threads, 2 attachments
- FTS5 index populated for search tests
- Use `open_db_sync()` pattern

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 18+ tests pass
- Tests use real DB (not mocked)
- FTS tests verify actual FTS5 functionality
- Edge cases (empty thread, no results, first/last message) covered

 {"id":"br-2rzb","title":"T1.2: Implement gate runner engine (subprocess execution, timing, output capture)","description":"## Objective
Implement the core gate runner engine that executes quality gates for `am ci` with deterministic behavior and structured capture.

## Work
- Build subprocess orchestration for sequential gate execution with explicit timeout/error handling.
- Capture exit status, stdout/stderr streams, and timing metrics per gate.
- Normalize runner outcomes into typed `GateResult` records for report generation.

## Deliverable
A robust execution engine that replaces shell gate orchestration and feeds typed CI reports.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:16.841124665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:54:31.377772462Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:54:31.377749690Z","close_reason":"Implemented gate runner engine in ci.rs: run_gate(), run_gates(), GateRunnerConfig, GateRunnerError. Tests added. Note: mcp-agent-mail-server crate has pre-existing compile errors from other TUI work that block full test run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2rzb","depends_on_id":"br-2r1f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:12.510494318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"br-2rzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T1.2: Implement Gate Runner Engine

## What to build
The core engine that executes CI gates as subprocesses, captures output, times them,
and collects results. This replaces the `gate()` function in ci.sh (lines 78-116).

## Key behaviors
1. **Subprocess execution**: Use std::process::Command to run each gate's command
2. **Timing**: Use std::time::Instant for sub-millisecond precision (ci.sh uses date +%s = 1s granularity)
3. **Output capture**: Capture stdout+stderr separately. On failure, retain last 50 lines of stderr for the report.
4. **Status mapping**: exit code 0 = Pass, non-zero = Fail, skip_in_quick + quick mode = Skip
5. **Environment setup**: Set CARGO_TARGET_DIR, DATABASE_URL, STORAGE_ROOT, AGENT_NAME, HTTP_HOST/PORT/PATH (same defaults as ci.sh lines 19-25)
6. **Working directory**: All gates run from project root
7. **Sequential by default**: Gates run one-at-a-time (parallel is T1.5)

## Function signature
```rust
fn run_gate(config: &GateConfig, mode: RunMode) -> GateResult
fn run_gates(gates: &[GateConfig], mode: RunMode) -> Vec<GateResult>
```

## Error handling
- A gate that fails to spawn (binary not found) should produce a Fail result with
  the spawn error in stderr_tail, not panic.
- Signal-killed processes (OOM, timeout) should be reported as Fail with appropriate detail.

## Implementation notes
- Use Command::output() for blocking execution with captured stdio
- Set current_dir to repo root (detected via git rev-parse --show-toplevel or cargo metadata)
- Duration is stored as std::time::Duration, serialized as f64 seconds in JSON

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (run_gate, run_gates functions)

## Reference
scripts/ci.sh lines 78-116 (gate function), lines 142-171 (gate invocations)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:28:09Z"},{"id":281,"issue_id":"br-2rzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T1.2 Gate Runner — skip_gate() semantics

The gate runner must handle TWO modes of execution:

1. gate(category, name, ...command): Runs the command, captures timing + exit code
   - Status: "pass" if exit code 0, "fail" otherwise
   - NDJSON entry: {category, name, status, elapsed_seconds, command}

2. skip_gate(category, name, reason): Records a gate as skipped WITHOUT running it
   - Status: "skip"
   - elapsed_seconds: 0
   - command: the REASON string (not the actual command)
   - Example: skip_gate("quality", "E2E dual-mode", "skipped in --quick mode")
     → {"category":"quality","name":"E2E dual-mode","status":"skip","elapsed_seconds":0,"command":"skipped in --quick mode"}

The skip_gate() function is critical for --quick mode where 4 E2E gates are skipped.
The GateResult struct should have a variant or field to distinguish:
  enum GateStatus { Pass, Fail, Skip }

Skipped gates affect the report:
- They count toward summary.skip (not pass or fail)
- They DON'T count toward thresholds.*.required_gates
- observed_pass_rate = passes / (total - skips), or null if (total - skips) = 0

-{"id":"br-3lc7f","title":"T10.8: Execute cross-platform validation matrix for all migrated native commands (Linux/macOS/Windows)","description":"## Objective
Establish an explicit cross-platform acceptance matrix for all migrated native commands so portability claims are proven, not assumed.

## Work
- Define OS matrix coverage for Linux, macOS, and Windows environments (or documented equivalent CI images).
- Validate each migrated command path (`am ci`, `am flake-triage`, `am bench`, `am check-inbox`, serve enhancements, `am golden`, share wizard, verify-live, native E2E harness).
- Stress platform-sensitive behavior: path normalization, newline/encoding differences, process spawning, lock semantics, env-file handling, and shell quoting assumptions.
- Capture deterministic per-OS artifacts and troubleshooting bundles.
- Open follow-up beads for any platform-specific gaps discovered.

## Deliverable
A reproducible cross-platform evidence set that confirms migrated native workflows are truly portable for end users.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix execution covers every migrated command surface in this epic across Linux/macOS/Windows.
- Platform-sensitive edge cases are explicitly exercised and documented (filesystem, process, network, env, and terminal behavior).
- Per-OS runs emit machine-readable evidence bundles (JSON summaries, command traces, stdout/stderr, timing, environment fingerprint).
- Any failures produce actionable follow-up beads with dependency links back to affected migration tracks.
- Final epic audit (`T10.6`) consumes this matrix as mandatory closure evidence.\n\n

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:37:51.689000209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T22:32:30.868562588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-1nbs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:53.569474309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-20qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:55.021552415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-246y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:55.261096845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-2azg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:54.504825102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-39eh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:54.772774106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-3ddq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:54.211115645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-3lyw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:53.922343078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-84gq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:53.005109598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3lc7f","depends_on_id":"br-h1yu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:53.261525456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3lyw","title":"Track 4: am check-inbox — Native inbox hook (replaces legacy/hooks/check_inbox.sh)","description":"## Purpose
Replace `legacy/hooks/check_inbox.sh` with native `am check-inbox` for reliable, rate-limited inbox polling with typed output and no curl/python dependency.

## Scope
- Rate-limited polling logic with concurrency-safe lock behavior.
- HTTP JSON-RPC and optional direct DB path for co-located usage.
- CLI options for agent selection, output mode, and direct mode.
- Comprehensive tests and deprecation messaging for legacy hook script.

## Why this matters
This path runs inside developer/editor workflows; reliability and low-noise diagnostics materially improve user experience.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- `am check-inbox` supports configured rate limiting, HTTP path, and direct DB path with deterministic behavior.
- Output and errors are structured for both humans and automation (`--json`), including actionable remediation context.
- Unit + integration + e2e coverage includes contention/race scenarios, transport failures, and auth/config edge cases.
- Logging captures timing, polling decisions, and request/result context needed for rapid troubleshooting.
- Legacy hook script is marked compatibility/deprecated after native parity verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:51.047797797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:44:24.327574672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T05:44:24.327558803Z","close_reason":"Completed: native command paths validated; legacy shims documented/deprecated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3lyw","depends_on_id":"br-2kml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:37:25.168405106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"br-3lyw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 4: am check-inbox — Native Inbox Hook

## What it replaces
legacy/hooks/check_inbox.sh (117 lines of bash)

## Current behavior
Designed to be called from git hooks or editor integrations. It:
1. Rate-limits checks: reads last-check timestamp from a file, skips if < 30s elapsed
2. Calls the MCP server's HTTP endpoint via curl to check for unread messages
3. Parses the JSON response with python3 inline one-liners
4. Formats and prints a summary (unread count, latest subject, sender)

## External dependencies eliminated
- **curl**: HTTP requests handled by asupersync::http::h1::HttpClient (already in project)
- **python3**: JSON parsing done by serde_json

## Key improvements
1. **Atomic rate limiting**: Current file-based timestamp tracking has race conditions
   (multiple concurrent hook invocations can all read stale timestamp). Rust can use
   file locking (flock) or atomic operations for correctness.
2. **Single binary**: Instead of curl | python3, it's one `am check-inbox` call with
   zero external deps.
3. **Configurable rate limit**: --rate-limit flag with duration parsing (30s, 1m, etc.)
4. **Structured output**: --json flag for machine consumption (e.g., editor plugins).
5. **Direct DB access option**: When the am binary has DB access (same machine as server),
   skip HTTP entirely and query the DB directly — instant results with no network.

## CLI interface
```
am check-inbox [--agent NAME] [--rate-limit 30s] [--json] [--direct]
```
--direct: Query DB directly instead of HTTP (for co-located setups)

## Implementation location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/check_inbox.rs (new module)
Wire into Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

## Files to read for context
- legacy/hooks/check_inbox.sh (the script being replaced)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (CLI structure)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs (for direct DB queries)

 {"id":"br-3m7xo","title":"F.2: Benchmark frame times + golden snapshot verification","description":"**Background**

After adding layout caching (F.1), we need to verify the performance improvement and ensure visual output is unchanged.

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

1. **Frame time benchmark** in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/benches/widget_bench.rs`:
   - `bench_heatmap_stable_100_frames` -- render a 20x20 heatmap 100 times with unchanged data. Measure total time.
   - `bench_heatmap_changing_100_frames` -- render with data changing every frame. Measure total time.
   - `bench_focus_ring_stable_100_frames` -- render focus ring 100 times with same area.
   - Compare cached vs uncached (use feature flag to toggle).

2. **Golden snapshot**: Render a HeatmapGrid with known data (5x5 grid, values 0-24, row labels A-E, col labels 1-5) to a ftui Buffer. Serialize the buffer contents as a JSON fixture. Test verifies the buffer matches the fixture exactly.

3. **A/B comparison**: Render the same data with and without caching. Verify the buffer contents are identical (pixel-perfect match).

**Tests (4 required)**

1. `golden_heatmap_5x5` -- render 5x5 heatmap to buffer, verify matches golden fixture
2. `cached_vs_uncached_identical` -- render with and without cache, verify buffer equality
3. `bench_stable_frames_faster_with_cache` -- 100 stable frames with cache < 100 without (assert ratio > 1.5x)
4. `bench_changing_frames_same_cost` -- changing frames have similar cost with/without cache","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Benchmark suite compares cached vs uncached rendering across at least four scenarios
- Golden snapshots cover canonical layouts and detect semantic or pixel-level drift
- Unit tests verify snapshot normalization and benchmark harness stability
- Integration test validates identical user-visible output under cached/uncached modes
- E2E PTY scenario confirms no visual regressions in live screen transitions
- Stable-frame performance gain is measurable and documented with reproducible artifacts
- Diagnostics include snapshot diff summaries, benchmark variance, and replay command metadata","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**FIX: Add focus ring golden snapshot.** Only the heatmap gets a golden fixture. The focus ring should also have a pixel-perfect golden test.

**FIX: Resize sequence test.** Render at 80x24, resize to 120x40, resize back to 80x24 — the cache should produce identical output to the first render. This validates cache invalidation correctness.

**FIX: Feature flag toggle.** The "compare cached vs uncached" test needs the feature flag toggle mechanism from F.1. If F.1 uses a runtime flag (not compile-time), this test can toggle it. If compile-time, need cfg-gated test variants.

**Additional test:**
5. `golden_focus_ring_snapshot` — render focus ring at known Rect, compare buffer to golden fixture
6. `resize_roundtrip_identical` — render → resize → render → resize back → render, compare first and third outputs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:45:30.518790701Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:45:30.518766726Z","close_reason":"F.2 complete: Created benches/widget_bench.rs with 5 criterion benchmarks (stable cached, changing, uncached, focus ring, golden 5x5). Added 4 unit tests: golden_heatmap_5x5 (determinism), cached_vs_uncached_identical (pixel-perfect A/B), bench_stable_frames_faster_with_cache (compute_count 1 vs 100), bench_changing_frames_same_cost. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3m7xo","depends_on_id":"br-1orm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:47:18.863241848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3mbli","title":"T15.3: Master E2E test orchestrator for TUI V3","description":"Orchestrator script that runs ALL TUI V3 E2E test scripts in sequence and produces a combined report.

This script calls:
- tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_charts.sh (T15.4: charts, canvas, heatmap)
- tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_rendering.sh (T15.5: markdown, tree, log viewer)
- tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_interaction.sh (T15.6: forms, clipboard, themes, error boundaries)

ORCHESTRATOR RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. Start server once (shared across all sub-scripts)
2. Run sub-scripts sequentially
3. Collect assertion counts from each sub-script
4. Produce combined summary:
   - Total assertions: N
   - Passed: N
   - Failed: N
   - Per-script breakdown
5. Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
6. Archive all diagnostic artifacts to tests/artifacts_native/tui_v3/

LOGGING:
- Timestamped log file: tests/artifacts_native/tui_v3/run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log
- Per-script logs preserved as sub-files
- On failure: full frame buffer dumps, event ring buffer snapshots, theme state
- Total run time per script reported

Target: 50+ total assertions across all sub-scripts (7+10+15 = 32 minimum, plus orchestrator assertions).

FILES: tests/e2e/test_tui_v3.sh","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 30+ E2E assertions

- [ ] Logging captures diagnostic artifacts on failure
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on failure
